skill.name: testing-test-writing
skill.description: Guides assistants to write focused Pest tests for Laravel applications, especially feature tests, unit tests, and critical user flows.
skill.summary: Write focused Pest tests for core workflows and business logic.
skill.author: DevanB

security_audit.summary: Static external-command and weak-cryptography alerts are false positives caused by Markdown text and testing terminology. The relative link on line 28 uses parent-directory traversal and asks the assistant to load a file outside the skill package. No prompt injection, network access, scripts, or credential handling evidence was found.

medium_findings.title: Out-of-Package File Reference
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to refer to a Markdown link that traverses parent directories. This can make the skill depend on unscanned content outside the packaged skill boundary.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory traversal is directly visible in the referenced Markdown link. The impact is moderate because it is a file reference, not executable code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static alerts on line 12 are Markdown formatting around test directory names. No shell command, interpolation, or execution behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains inline code formatting for directory names only. There is no Ruby code, shell command, or command execution context.

low_findings.title: Testing Text Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts point to prose about testing, Claude Code guidance, and Pest tests. No cryptographic API or weak algorithm usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural-language instructions and do not call MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar APIs. The scanner likely matched unrelated substrings.

dangerous_patterns.title: Parent Directory Traversal in Documentation Link
dangerous_patterns.description: The Markdown link uses parent-directory traversal to reference content outside the skill package. This weakens review boundaries for marketplace publication.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path traversal sequence is present in the link target. The concern is boundary bypass, not direct code execution.

content.user_title: Write Focused Pest Tests for Critical Paths
content.value_statement: Teams often miss critical behavior when tests are broad, slow, or tied to implementation details. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create focused Pest tests for real workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides Pest test creation for feature and unit test directories.
Prioritizes core user workflows and critical business paths.
Encourages descriptive test names that explain expected behavior.
Supports mocking of databases, APIs, file systems, and services.
Recommends Pest datasets for repeated test scenarios.
Helps decide when test coverage is needed at completion points.
limitations: Does not include executable test helpers or framework code.
Depends on Pest and Laravel project conventions.
References an external standards file outside this skill package.
Does not guarantee complete coverage for every edge case.

use_cases.title: Cover a New Feature
use_cases.description: Create focused feature tests for a new workflow before merging the implementation.

use_cases.title: Validate Business Logic
use_cases.description: Add fast unit tests around rules, calculations, and validation behavior.

use_cases.title: Review Release Readiness
use_cases.description: Identify critical paths that need tests before a release or handoff.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Pest Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add Pest tests for the main success path of this feature.

prompt_templates.title: Test Validation Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to write Pest tests for validation rules, failure states, and clear user-facing outcomes.

prompt_templates.title: Mock External Services
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to test behavior while mocking external APIs, file systems, or service dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Critical Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review the changed feature and list the critical Pest tests needed before completion.

output_examples.output: A concise Pest feature test plan covering successful registration, validation failures, and expected persistence.

output_examples.input: Test payment retry logic without calling the provider.
output_examples.output: A behavior-focused unit test outline using a mocked payment service and clear assertions for retry outcomes.

output_examples.input: Decide what needs coverage before release.
output_examples.output: A prioritized list of critical workflows, recommended test types, and skipped low-value edge cases.

best_practices: Focus tests on observable behavior and business outcomes.
Keep unit tests fast and isolate external dependencies with mocks.
Use descriptive test names that state the expected result.
anti_patterns: Do not test private implementation details instead of outcomes.
Do not add broad slow tests for every minor utility.
Do not call real external services from routine test runs.

faq.question: What framework does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Pest tests in Laravel projects.

faq.question: Can it write both feature and unit tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives guidance for tests in feature and unit test directories.

faq.question: Does it run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides test writing and recommends running tests before committing changes.

faq.question: Does it require external services?
faq.answer: No. It recommends mocking external services during tests.

faq.question: Is every edge case required?
faq.answer: No. The skill prioritizes critical workflows and business behavior.

faq.question: What security warning applies?
faq.answer: The skill references a file outside its package, so reviewers should verify that dependency.
